title: Quiz 3 and Lumen Lighting

video.note: Covers Lumen as Unreal Engine 5 dynamic global illumination, light bounce, color bleed, static baked light maps, broken static lighting when objects move, movable lights, Lumen on/off comparison, source radius, softer shadows, and emissive material lighting. Includes Quiz 3 as the opening checkpoint.

learningTarget: I can explain the basic purpose of Lumen and use lighting evidence to show how global illumination changes an Unreal scene.

bellRinger.prompt: What makes lighting in a 3D scene feel realistic instead of flat?

vocabulary.definition: Unreal Engine 5 system for dynamic global illumination and reflections.
Light that bounces around a scene instead of stopping after one hit.
Light or scene information that appears on a shiny surface.
Light that reaches an area after bouncing from another surface.
Lighting that updates while objects or lights move.
Camera brightness control that changes how light or dark a scene appears.
A darker area created when light is blocked.
Screenshots or notes that prove how lighting changed the scene.
A baked texture that stores static lighting information.
A light that can update dynamically instead of relying on baked lighting.

assignment.title: Lumen Lighting Comparison
evidenceRequired: Quiz 3 completion record or teacher checkoff.
Google Drive link to a screenshot showing a lighting setup before adjustment.
Google Drive link to a screenshot showing improved Lumen or global illumination result.
Short reflection explaining how lighting changed the scene.

exitTicket: What is one thing Lumen helps simulate, and how can you prove it with evidence?
